name: context-engineering-kit-scm
description: Hand-crafted collection of advanced context engineering techniques and patterns with minimal token footprint focused on improving agent result quality.

plugins.name: reflexion
plugins.description: Collection of commands that force LLM to reflect on previous response and output. Based on papers like Self-Refine and Reflexion. These techniques improve the output of large language models by introducing feedback and refinement loops.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Introduce codebase and PR review commands and skills using multiple specialized agents.

plugins.name: git
plugins.description: Introduces commands for commit and PRs creation, plus skills for git worktrees and notes.

plugins.name: tdd
plugins.description: Introduces commands for test-driven development, common anti-patterns and skills for testing using subagents.

plugins.name: sadd
plugins.description: Introduces skills for subagent-driven development, dispatches fresh subagent for each task with code review between tasks, enabling fast iteration with quality gates.

plugins.name: ddd
plugins.description: Introduces command to update CLAUDE.md with best practices for domain-driven development, focused on quality of code, includes Clean Architecture, SOLID principles, and other design patterns.

plugins.name: sdd
plugins.description: Specification Driven Development workflow commands and agents, based on Github Spec Kit and OpenSpec. Uses specialized agents for effective context management and quality review.

plugins.name: kaizen
plugins.description: Inspired by Japanese continuous improvement philosophy, Agile and Lean development practices. Introduces commands for analysis of root cause of issues and problems, including 5 Whys, Cause and Effect Analysis, and other techniques.

plugins.name: customaize-agent
plugins.description: Commands and skills for writing and refining commands, hooks, skills for Claude Code, includes Anthropic Best Practices and Agent Persuasion Principles that can be useful for sub-agent workflows.

plugins.name: docs
plugins.description: Commands for analysing project, writing and refining documentation.

plugins.name: mcp
plugins.description: Commands for setup well known MCP server integration if needed and update CLAUDE.md file with requirement to use this MCP server for current project.

plugins.name: rules
plugins.description: Project-scoped coding rules. Slash commands emit .claude/rules/<topic>.md with stack-aware globs inferred from the project, and register a pointer under ## Agent docs in CLAUDE.md. Includes /rules:setup-* for ~25 rules (SOLID/YAGNI/DRY/KISS/DDD/design-patterns/frontend/api-design/typescript-bp/dotnet8-bp/dotnet10-bp), /rules:list, /rules:doctor, /rules:uninstall. Rules referencing skills prompt before embedding the reference.

plugins.name: skills
plugins.description: Per-project skill installer. Slash commands copy 10 curated skills (dotnet-expert, design-pattern-advisor, frontend-design, api-design, angular-signals, rxjs-patterns-for-angular, clean-architecture, project-md-generator, project-rules-generator, graphify) into <project>/.claude/skills/<name>/ so they activate only where you want them. Includes /skills:list and /skills:uninstall.

plugins.name: fpf
plugins.description: First Principles Framework (FPF) for structured reasoning. Implements ADI (Abduction-Deduction-Induction) cycle for hypothesis generation, logical verification, empirical validation, and auditable decision-making.
